audit_items.proof_trace: In Start(), if _maintain_thread_group->start() fails (L86), the code resets _maintain_thread_group to null (L93) but lacks a 'return false' — execution falls through. If _work_thread_group->start() and all subsequent operations (ResolveAddress, start_listen) succeed, execution reaches L156 where _maintain_thread_group->io_service() is called unconditionally, dereferencing the null pointer. All other failure branches (L101, L125, L146) properly return false; this missing guard is the sole exception.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker who can cause OS-level resource exhaustion (e.g., thread creation failure via ulimit or memory pressure) at server startup could trigger _maintain_thread_group->start() to fail while _work_thread_group->start() and network binding succeed, resulting in a null pointer dereference (SIGSEGV) and process crash (DoS).
